does: AdaptiveGuard introduces an adaptive guardrail system that protects LLM-powered applications by detecting novel jailbreak attacks as out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs and learning to defend against them through continual learning. The core contribution addresses the dramatic performance degradation of existing guardrails when encountering unseen attack patterns—dropping from 95% accuracy to as low as 12% in preliminary analysis. Rather than relying on static rule-based or pre-trained defense mechanisms, AdaptiveGuard uses OOD detection to identify novel attacks in real-time and employs continual learning to incrementally update its defensive capabilities post-deployment, enabling the guardrail to evolve as new jailbreak techniques emerge.
connects: This work builds on a growing body of research addressing the vulnerability of LLMs to prompt injection and jailbreak attacks, extending beyond foundational guardrailing approaches like LlamaGuard and similar static filtering systems. It connects to prior work on OOD detection in machine learning, which has long tackled the problem of identifying inputs that diverge from training distributions. The paper also draws on continual or incremental learning literature, which addresses how systems can update their models over time without catastrophic forgetting. AdaptiveGuard synthesizes these threads to tackle a specific software engineering challenge: the reality that guardrails deployed in production face evolving threat landscapes, extending earlier work that treated guardrail performance as a one-time snapshot rather than an ongoing adaptation problem.
enables: This work enables more robust long-term deployment of LLM-powered systems by establishing a framework for runtime safety that adapts to emerging threats. It points toward future research on automated detection and response to zero-day jailbreak attacks, where systems can identify novel attack patterns without human-in-the-loop labeling. The continual learning approach suggests future work on efficient, privacy-preserving model updates in distributed LLM applications, and on understanding which jailbreak characteristics are most informative for OOD detection. More broadly, AdaptiveGuard enables exploration of automated guardrail improvement pipelines and raises questions about feedback mechanisms, safety guarantees during learning, and how to prioritize which emerging threats to defend against in resource-constrained post-deployment settings.
